text: Presenting a case of laparoscopic myomectomy in multiple myoma. A novel technique of suturing with a single barb suture is shown. I am Professor Alka Kiplani. I bring you greetings from Paras Hospital's Gurgaon.

During laparoscopic examination, multiple myomas are visible on the enlarged uterus, resembling a fourteen to sixteen weeks pregnancy size. Methylene blue dye is used for chromotubation, confirming the patency of bilateral fallopian tubes.

The surgeon begins the myomectomy by making an incision on a large sub-serosal fibroid using either a harmonic scalpel or scissors. Pitressin injection is administered to minimize bleeding. Scissors are used to achieve a precise dissection plane within the myoma capsule. The surgeon palpates and removes each myoma, using a claw instrument for stabilization. Removed myomas are carefully deposited in the pouch of Douglas or paracolic gutter to prevent loss in the peritoneal cavity, ensuring all are accounted for.
The surgeon uses a single barb suture, number zero V-Loc, to suture all the myomas subserosally from anterior to posterior, completing the suturing process in a single continuous motion.
